title: Structure of the Uniface Record
text: Structure of the Uniface Record Uniface distinguishes between the fixed part and the variable part of the record. The fixed part consists of fields which are a one-to-one representation of fields in the modeled entity. The variable part consists of one field (possibly continued in overflow records) that contains one or more fields at the model level. The contents of the variable part are automatically handled by Uniface. With the exception of segmented fields for handling large amounts of data, such as BLOBs, the database connector does not need to know about this record structure. Variable-Length Packing Codes Fields in the fixed part of the record are treated as fixed-length fields by Uniface internally, although they may be variable-length fields in the database. Uniface provides special model-level packing codes (VC1 through VC n , VU1 through VU n , and VR1 through VR n ) to indicate a field in the fixed part of the record which must be stored as a variable-length field in the database when possible. Uniface automatically translates these packing codes to the connector-level packing code pC, pU or pR, with the following descriptions governing the characteristics of the field: The field ufmax in the field list is set to the maximum length The variable-length property (mask value 4) is set in the field ufbits in the field list Uniface automatically handles trailing spaces with variable-length fields in the fixed part of the record. Related Links Variable-Length Packing Codes Variable-length packing codes work with DBMSs that support a VARCHAR data type. They make it possible to store only the actual part of the field that is filled in, and not the trailing spaces that would be added for a fixed-length field. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
